response.justification: Wei Chen is a polyglot generalist with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background and broad full-stack experience, but the role demands deep Java and Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on Spring ecosystem work — none of which appear meaningfully in this resume. Java is listed as a skill and surfaces briefly at Altom (robotic arms) and NI LabWindows (C IDE in C/C++), but there is no enterprise Java development, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate exposure, no Kotlin, and no JVM-focused work anywhere in the history. The candidate's IDE-adjacent experience (NI LabWindows) is in C and predates most of their career; it does not translate to JVM IDE internals. The profile is technically accomplished but fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this position.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows/CVI, MCP code generators, test frameworks Trial and Fluent-Asserts)
Broad systems experience across embedded, mobile, web, and high-throughput microservices signals adaptability and engineering depth
Active open-source contributor and public communicator (talks, workshops, OSS projects) reflecting strong collaboration and knowledge-sharing habits
concerns: No demonstrable Java or Kotlin expertise; primary language throughout career is TypeScript/Node.js with Java mentioned only incidentally
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience present anywhere in the resume — the central technical requirement of the role is entirely absent
No JVM internals knowledge evidenced; IDE experience is limited to C tooling at NI, which does not map to JetBrains-style JVM IDE development

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
